name: Ewa Gentry
desc: Planned subdivision with community pools and playgrounds — clean, family-friendly, tight-knit suburban living.
insider: Community pools are the social hub — most subdivisions have their own pool and the neighborhood swim programs for kids are excellent
The homes look similar from the outside but the neighborhood is genuinely tight-knit — expect welcome baskets from neighbors when you move in
Street names follow themes by subdivision (flowers, fish, birds) — it makes giving directions surprisingly easy once you know the pattern
Playground hopping with kids is a real weekend pastime — there are over a dozen well-maintained parks within walking distance
HOA fees range from $50-150/month depending on the subdivision — they cover pool maintenance, common areas, and landscaping
The annual Ewa by Gentry community garage sale draws shoppers from all over West Oahu — prices are genuinely good
Parking is tight in the narrower cul-de-sacs — if you have more than two cars, you'll be jockeying for street spots
Geiger Community Park has a popular dog park and basketball courts — it gets busy on weekend mornings
The walk to Fort Weaver Road for shopping is doable but not pleasant in the heat — you'll want a car for errands
This is one of the more affordable places to buy a single-family home on Oahu — expect to be around $700K-900K for a 3-bedroom
